opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My chest feels incredibly tight, I can't catch a deep breath, and I feel like I am going to pass out if I sit back.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Rapidly worsening breathlessness and lightheadedness in the context of recently diagnosed pericarditis.

symptom_narrative.onset: rapid deterioration over the last 12 hours
symptom_narrative.character: the pain has changed from sharp and stabbing to a constant, heavy pressure
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: extreme lightheadedness, feeling 'fuzzy' and confused, cold hands and feet
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: diagnosed with viral pericarditis 5 days ago, was told it was sharp pain that improved with sitting forward and anti-inflammatories
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: sitting on the edge of the couch leaning forward, unable to mobilise

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: taking regularly for pericarditis over the past 5 days

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: spouse present and very concerned, patient diagnosed with viral pericarditis 5 days ago

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: frightened that this is just her pericarditis getting worse and wants to know if she should just take more medication

access_barriers.transport: spouse present but patient cannot safely mobilise or be transported by private car given hypotension risk
